behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection + CLI parsing/output across subprocess boundaries. MOCK-MISS: this is not just one pure function if rename status comes from git diff/name-status. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss real rename heuristics and status codes from git, so a mocked test could pass while real repos fail. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text (git/CLI categorization only, no AI/ML decision).
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it with git, run the CLI against that repo, and assert stdout report has the file only in renamed count/list and not in added/deleted.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real git diff/subprocess output and real binary content handling (encoding, binary markers, exit behavior). REAL-INFRA is explicit (git repo + subprocess). MOCK-HIDE: stubbed diff text may not reproduce true binary diff behavior, so crash bugs can be hidden. Rejected higher levels: no model output or multi-agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, commit baseline, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This edge case is driven by real git repository state and real command behavior in an unborn HEAD repo. MOCK-HIDE: fake repo adapters often skip exact git error/empty semantics, hiding failures. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is single CLI/git scenario, no AI/ML and no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero/empty structured report on stdout and zero exit code (or defined non-error behavior).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires end-to-end correctness against real git history, including status transitions that occur only through actual repository operations. REAL-INFRA applies (real repo + git process). MOCK-HIDE: synthetic fixtures can align with implementation assumptions and miss real-history mismatches. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output quality or multi-agent sequencing in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing known numbers of added/deleted/renamed/modified files across commits, run the CLI for the target range, and assert reported counts exactly match ground truth derived from git history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is git index vs working tree semantics, which are OS/repo state + git command behavior, not just local function logic. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): yes, needs real git state transitions (staged/unstaged) so Integration is too low. Rejected higher levels: behavior text concerns CLI parity across git states only, with no AI/ML or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, make controlled edits; run CLI with changes unstaged, capture stdout; stage same changes, rerun, and assert structured output is identical.
